dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 responding for sick person in Richmond park area.
Hi, I'm Ryan, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I think I had a seizure. I don't remember how I got here.
Can you tell me your name?
Yes, it's Asha.
Good. What town or area are we in?
Henrico.
And what did you say happened before you got here?
I think I had a seizure or something. I was drinking and just... don't know.
Can you follow my finger with your eyes?
Yes, I can.
Can you repeat back what I say?
Go ahead.
The sun is bright.
The sun is bright.
Squeeze my hand on three. One... two... three.
How long have you been feeling confused?
I don't know, maybe two hours?
Can I take a look at your throat or check your airway?
Go ahead.
Resp rate is 24, breathing even. SPO2 is 96% on room air.
Are you wheezing or coughing at all?
No. But I can feel it even so.
Pulse is 105, BP is 132 over 81.
What would you rate the pain in your legs and belly on a scale of 0 to 10?
A 10 all over, mainly in my legs and belly.
Have you had any alcohol recently?
Yes, I had a beer maybe 10 minutes ago.
Are you experiencing any other symptoms like numbness, tingling, or weakness?
Just the belly and leg pain. I don't feel weak though.
Do you have any allergies to medications or other substances?
No known drug allergies. Tell them to try to help me, okay?
Do you have any current medications you're on?
I don't take any at the moment, nope.
Have you had a history of seizures or other similar events?
Yes, when I drink too much or too little. I also had pancreatitis. I also deal with depression and PTSD. It's been hard for years now.
How long ago did you eat or drink?
I had a beer maybe 10 minutes ago. I don't eat much these days, but I mostly drink when I get stress or lonely or just need something to distract me from my problems.
Can you describe how you ended up sitting on the park bench?

I really don't know. I might have walked here after I blacked out or something. I could have gotten disoriented or just wandered out because I didn't know where else to go at that point. It happens sometimes to me, ya know.
On a scale of 1 to 10, what's your current pain level?
Ten, all over-literal fire in my belly and some tight stuff in the legs.
Is the pain sharp, burning, throbbing, or something else?
It's on fire in my belly and like ziplocked in my legs. I don't really know how else to describe that one.
Does the pain spread to your chest, arms, or other places?
No, it's all local in the legs and belly. Maybe some radiating into the back, but I'm just not sure. It could be my imagination, maybe the stress is making it feel like that?
Patient is alert and oriented, with a GCS of 15-A&Ox3. She knows who she is and where she is, but can't recall events leading into the park.
Are you in pain anywhere else, like your back or chest? Any bruising or rashes or bites or signs of irritation on your skin, legs, anywhere?

No. It's all legs and belly. The rest feels fine, I guess. I don't see anything on my skin that looks wrong-at least not yet. Sometimes, I get these rashes from drinking too much. I see them sometimes before I pass out.
No signs of head trauma. Neck is mobile without pain. You don't have any stiffness or issues trying to move it or hold your head up?
You have movement in all extremities without difficulty. No deformities or numbness or any loss of motion or feeling in your spine or back.
Clear and equal breath sounds bilaterally. No wheeze or crackles. I could listen to your heart and it's clear. Any soreness in the chest or upper body when you take a breath or move at all?
Your stomach, does it feel swollen or tender? You said your belly feels tight-anything sticking out or looking abnormal otherwise or just pain and tension in it?

I feel like it's tight with pain. It doesn't look swollen or distended, but I can feel it moving around a little. I mean, my arms are fine, but I guess my legs are like the same story in terms of bloating or something.
Pulse is 105. It's consistent throughout the evaluation so far. Your heart seems to be working fine here, even with the stress and activity.
Respirations are 24, a bit fast but even. You don't look like you're struggling too badly.
BP is 132 over 81. You don't look like you're in shock or anything from what I can tell at the moment given your skin and color.
SpO2 is 96 on room air. You're not reliant on oxygen, so we'll go with room air for now.
BSL is 89 via fingerstick. You're not able to measure this with an IV yet because we haven't started one, but you aren't hypoglycemic here based on other symptoms and your behavior.
EKG strip is sinus tachycardia. The rhythm is stable and your heart seems to match your breathing a bit. Nothing looks too off-kilter from what we're seeing here.

I am now going to start an IV on you for fluids and medication. You'll feel a small pinch when I insert the line, but it's quick-just like getting a shot sometimes. Don't worry, I'll keep talking to you to be gentle.
Would you mind if we place you on a cardiac monitor to check your heart rhythm from the same shoulder?
No, go ahead.
Strip is in. Rhythm is stable. Pulse is 105. Line is patent with good blood return.
I am now placing you on a 4-lead cardiac monitor. It's secured here and will keep track of your rhythm as you move around-don't need to stay completely still.
Based on your seizure history, alcohol intake, and ongoing leg and belly pain, we are following the medical-seizure protocol.
Pulse is up a bit to 111. It's still elevated, but steady. BP is now 130 over 71 and your overall color remains good.
SPO2 is still 96 on room air. No change in that so far. Your breathing is clear and equal.
BSL is now not detected. We'll re-test shortly or verify with the hospital again if needed-no immediate concerns in this case yet.
IV is still patent. You are now guarding your abdomen more than before and still rating pain as a 10 after reassessment.
Have your symptoms improved or stayed the same since we started treatment?
Still feels like a 10. I can't really see the pain getting lighter at this point, but I don't really expect it to change much for now. I just want someone to help me.
You mentioned you've had seizures when drinking-has that happened often? How often is this event in your experience?

Yes, it's happened a few times. Sometimes late at night, sometimes after heavy drinking or when I'm not drinking at all. I get confused and sometimes lose control-I can't stay awake properly. I feel very lost at times.
Would you prefer to go to Chippenham Hospital?
Yes, go there.
We'll now secure you to the stretcher for transport. Are you okay with that?
Yes, go ahead.
We noticed you mentioned alcohol use and some emotional concerns. Are you currently thinking about hurting yourself in any way at this time?

I know I need to change, but it's hard. I just don't want to live like this as it is and wish these conditions would end the pain for me, but I know it's not right and I have no suicide plan or any desire to follow through with any.
Would the hospital like any documents like ID or insurance from you?
No. I can sign for myself.
Stretcher is clear and we're en route to Chippenham.
